narrative:
DRIVER
USD fades on H4 CSM (44 vs D1 100) — the most important short-term signal in this brief — while CHF (H4 100 vs D1 33) and AUD (H4 70 vs D1 31) accelerate hard against the D1 Risk-Off High regime, flagging an intraday structural rotation away from dollar dominance. USDCHF H4 Sell with CHF surging is the cleanest expression; AUDUSD and NZDUSD H4 Sells conflict with CHF/AUD H4 CSM strength, so AUDCHF long is the correlation-pure trade, exploiting AUDUSD-NZDUSD 0.88 cluster without USD noise. GBPJPY D1+H4 Strong Buy with GBPUSD-GBPJPY 0.81 correlation confirms GBP is the outlier laggard that can still run via JPY cross.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: None
Fading: None

WATCH
Fed Chair Warsh commentary on policy divergence hits USD positioning; USDCAD long faces direct headwind if USD fading accelerates through 44 H4 CSM.
Copper extended 1.95% rally continuation lifts AUDUSD; a second session close above triggers AUD H4 CSM follow-through.
US 10Y yield at 4.558 dropping further compresses USDJPY; D1+H4 Buy on USDJPY unwinds if 10Y breaks 4.50.

themes.theme: Domestic equity market focus with limited macro content
Fed Chair Warsh policy uncertainty and potential policy divergence
Mixed economic signals - corporate earnings strength vs macro concerns
Equity volatility risk from policy uncertainty

key_observation: Headline flow dominated by equity research and corporate actions rather than macro FX drivers. Two notable mentions of Fed Chair Warsh policy concerns suggest market anxiety over potential hawkish stance or policy conflicts with Trump administration. No direct currency, rates, or international macro content detected.
watch: Fed Chair Warsh policy signals and potential Trump administration divergence. Equity market volatility could drive USD safe-haven flows if risk-off sentiment accelerates. Monitor for any earnings guidance that impacts growth expectations.
